name: CARDIAC INJURY
definition: most common type of cardiac injury after blunt thoracic trauma, but difficult to diagnose mildest form of cardiac injury without proved myocardial cell damage / morphologic cardiac herniation and volvulus

demographics.Frequency: 25% of traumatic deaths related to heart injury

discriminating_features: distinct boundary between normal + contused tissue
patchy necrosis + hemorrhage → eventually heal to form patchy and irregular fibrosis

general: distinct boundary between normal + contused tissue
patchy necrosis + hemorrhage → eventually heal to form patchy and irregular fibrosis
NUC: traumatic VSD typically occurs in the apical interventricular septum ← anteroposterior
extravasation of IV contrast into pericardial space
lung interposition between aorta + pulmonary artery / heart + diaphragm / RA + RVOT
contour abnormality of heart = deformed ventricular silhouette
cardiac displacement into either hemithoracic cavity
pericardial sac "empty" = delineated by air / filled with loops of gas-containing
leftward deviation of the myocardial septum
torsion along IVC + great vessels = strangulation of entire heart
pneumopericardium, esp. if associated with pneumothorax
pulmonary edema ← new onset heart failure
Aortic valve: tear / avulsion from annulus of aortic valve cusp (esp. noncoronary cusp)

clinical.Cx: arrhythmia, low cardiac output, ventricular septal defect, ventricular rupture,
clinical.Prognosis: 33% surgical mortality, excellent long-term prognosis in survivors
